response.justification: Strong match for geospatial domain expertise and distributed systems concepts, with demonstrated engineering rigor in problem-solving and quality assurance. However, the primary technical requirement (modern C++) has a 10+ year gap since last use (2014), with no Rust experience, and recent career trajectory shows shift away from systems programming entirely toward web/scripting languages. Would require substantial C++ ramp-up for a mid-senior individual contributor role.
strengths: Direct geospatial domain experience through GISCollective founder role and Green Map work
Proven distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with low-latency architecture
Strong engineering culture (TDD, testing, CI/CD, code quality focus) across multiple senior roles
concerns: C++ expertise is severely dated (last used 2014-2015, 10+ years ago) with no mention of Rust as alternative
Recent 5+ years dominated by TypeScript/Node.js/DLang—career trajectory shows drift away from systems programming
Background is primarily web/application engineer, not systems engineer—limited infrastructure/performance optimization at scale

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ expertise with no Rust alternative
Direct geospatial domain knowledge and GIS platform experience
Distributed systems and performance optimization background
